response.justification: The candidate is a deeply experienced engineer (~18 years across web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems) with a genuine open-source pedigree — founding an open-source geo-data platform, authoring D-language libraries, and speaking at FOSS4G — which fits the firm's open-source and digital-sovereignty focus well. However, the role demands minimum C1 German and the resume states only basic German, a hard disqualifier for a client-facing consulting leadership position. He also shows no formal team-leadership, people-management, or executive experience, and no stated UML, Python, or requirements-management background, all of which are core to this Head of Development role.
strengths: Extensive, versatile engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Strong open-source track record: founded an open-source GIS platform, maintains D-language libraries, FOSS4G speaker
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer through multi-day courses, workshops, and community dojos
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' versus the required minimum C1 for a client-facing German consulting role
No formal team-leadership, line-management, or executive experience despite the head-of-development scope
No evidence of UML, Python, requirements management, or formal IT-security/compliance work

key_factors.factor: Breadth of engineering and open-source expertise
Lack of formal leadership and architecture-governance track record
